latHint: Think decor and a mannequin's unblinking look.
pivHint: WATCH in this passage means stay alert, be careful. But WATCH can also mean simply looking at something intently, unable to look away. What's a stronger word for that?
winText: You found it. To WATCH out can mean caution, or it can mean to STARE - and Paul says WATCH out for each other in love, keeping a close eye out.
lossText: The word was STARE. WATCH was the pivot - staying alert in Galatians, and the intense kind of looking that can't look away.
votdVerse: For the entire law is fulfilled in keeping this one command: 'Love your neighbor as yourself.'
passageText: You, my brothers and sisters, were called to be free. But do not use your freedom to indulge the flesh; rather, serve one another humbly in love. For the entire law is fulfilled in keeping this one command: 'Love your neighbor as yourself.' If you bite and devour each other, watch out or you will be destroyed by each other.
